CREATE TABLE test_data (id INT, x FLOAT, y FLOAT);
INSERT INTO test_data (id, x, y) VALUES (1, 10.0, 20.0);
INSERT INTO test_data (id, x, y) VALUES (2, 15.0, 25.0);
INSERT INTO test_data (id, x, y) VALUES (3, NULL, 30.0);
INSERT INTO test_data (id, x, y) VALUES (4, 20.0, NULL);
INSERT INTO test_data (id, x, y) VALUES (5, NULL, NULL);
CREATE TABLE additional_data (id INT, value FLOAT);
SELECT REGR_SYY(x, y) FROM test_data WHERE x IS NOT NULL AND y IS NOT NULL;
SELECT REGR_SYY(td.x, ad.value) FROM test_data td JOIN additional_data ad ON td.id = ad.id WHERE td.x IS NOT NULL AND ad.value IS NOT NULL;
SELECT REGR_SYY(x, y) FROM (SELECT * FROM test_data WHERE y IS NOT NULL) AS filtered_data;
SELECT REGR_SYY(x, y) FROM (SELECT x, y FROM test_data WHERE x > 0) AS positive_x_data;
SELECT REGR_SYY(x, y) FROM test_data GROUP BY id HAVING COUNT(y) > 0;
SELECT REGR_SYY(x, y) FROM (SELECT x, y FROM test_data WHERE x < 15) AS limited_data WHERE y > 10;
SELECT REGR_SYY(td.x, ad.value) FROM test_data td LEFT JOIN additional_data ad ON td.id = ad.id WHERE td.y IS NOT NULL;
SELECT REGR_SYY(x, y) FROM test_data WHERE (x + y) > 40;
SELECT REGR_SYY(x, y) FROM (SELECT x, y FROM test_data ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 3) AS subset_data;
